text.title: Design Token Hierarchy and Structure
text.summary: Three-tier token model (primitive → semantic → component), naming rules, and JSON structure.

section.title: Three-Tier Model

rule.statement: Organize tokens in three tiers: primitive (raw values), semantic (purpose aliases), component (usage-specific). Never skip a tier by referencing primitives from components.
rule.rationale: Skipping tiers couples component styling to raw values. A brand color change then requires touching every component instead of one semantic token.

section.p: Tier definitions: Primitive = the raw value (color-blue-500: #3b82f6). Semantic = the purpose (color-action-primary references color-blue-500). Component = the slot (button-bg-primary references color-action-primary).

rule.statement: Primitive token names describe what the value is (color-blue-500, spacing-4, font-size-base). Semantic token names describe purpose, never a value (color-text-primary, color-bg-secondary). Component token names describe usage (button-padding-horizontal).
rule.rationale: Value-bearing names in semantic tokens (color-blue-primary) break when the brand color changes — the name becomes a lie.

antipattern.description: Using a value-bearing name as a semantic token: color-blue-500-action.
antipattern.reason: When brand blue changes to teal, the token name is wrong and callers must be renamed too.

rule.statement: Store all token tiers as JSON files in a tokens/ directory. Primitive values use string or numeric values. Semantic tokens reference primitives using {category.name} syntax. Generate all platform outputs (CSS, JS, iOS, Android) from this source — never edit generated files.
rule.rationale: A single JSON source of truth means one edit propagates to all platforms automatically.

example.note: Dark theme is a separate semantic-dark.json that overrides the same keys with different primitive references.

section.title: Token Organization

rule.statement: Store tokens in a tokens/ directory: primitive.json, semantic.json, semantic-dark.json, and a components/ subfolder for component-specific tokens. Version the token package separately so consumers adopt at their own pace.
rule.rationale: Separating light and dark themes as distinct files prevents merge conflicts and makes theme-switching logic explicit.

section.reference: Primitive token JSON with full color/spacing/typography/radius/shadow scales
Semantic token JSON with light-theme aliases and dark-theme extension pattern
